name: web_search
description: Search the web and return SERP results (title, URL, description) from the configured provider (searxng, duckduckgo, bing, tavily, google, zhipu, exa, or bocha). Use this to find pages relevant to a query before optionally scraping or extracting their content. Returns a list of results formatted as Title/URL/Description blocks.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 10, clamped 1-100).

categories.description: Result category (default 'general'). Honored by providers that support it (e.g. searxng, tavily topic).

timeRange.description: Restrict results to a recency window (default 'all').

engines.description: Provider-specific engine selector (e.g. searxng engine list, zhipu engine).

name: web_scrape
description: Fetch a single web page over HTTP and return its content in the requested formats. Conversion happens server-side with no browser and no JavaScript execution, so it is fast and safe. Every URL is validated by an SSRF guard that blocks loopback, private, and link-local addresses. Supports markdown, html, rawHtml, and links output. Screenshot and scripted-action formats are intentionally unsupported (they require a real browser).

url.description: The http/https URL to scrape. Must resolve to a public address unless the server was started with --allow-private-hosts.

formats.description: Content formats to return (default ['markdown']). 'links' returns absolute URLs found on the page; 'rawHtml' returns the unmodified HTML; 'extract' is an alias for 'markdown'.

onlyMainContent.description: When true, isolate the page's main/article region before conversion, dropping navigation and chrome (default false).

name: web_map
description: Discover URLs reachable from a starting URL, using the site's sitemap.xml and/or in-page HTML links. Useful for enumerating a site before targeted scraping. Results are restricted to the starting host (optionally including subdomains) and can be filtered by a search substring. SSRF-guarded.

search.description: Optional case-insensitive substring; only URLs containing it are returned.

ignoreSitemap.description: Skip sitemap.xml discovery and use only in-page HTML links (default false).

sitemapOnly.description: Use only sitemap.xml, ignore in-page HTML links (default false).

includeSubdomains.description: Include URLs from subdomains of the starting host (default false).

limit.description: Maximum number of URLs to return (default 5000).

name: web_extract
description: Fetch one or more URLs over HTTP and return their cleaned main content as markdown, concatenated with per-URL headers. URLs are fetched concurrently (bounded parallelism) and each is SSRF-guarded; a failure on one URL is reported inline without failing the others. Use this to gather readable content from a known set of pages.

urls.description: List of http/https URLs to fetch and extract.

name: web_fetch
description: Fetch a single web page and return its content as markdown. A simpler alternative to web_scrape for the common case — just provide a URL and get readable content back with no options required. Uses the efficient dom_to_markdown path (single HTML parse, no htmd). SSRF-guarded.

name: web_fetch_text
description: Fetch a single web page and return its content as plain text with all HTML tags stripped. Use this when you want raw text without any markdown formatting, for example to feed into another text-processing pipeline. Lighter than full markdown conversion.

name: web_fetch_headers
description: Fetch only the HTTP response headers and status code for a URL without downloading the body. Use this to check whether a page is accessible, redirected, or returns an error — much faster than a full fetch when you only need metadata about the resource.

name: web_search_scrape
description: Search the web and scrape the top N results into markdown in a single MCP call. This compound operation saves round trips compared to calling web_search then web_scrape for each result. URLs are fetched concurrently (bounded parallelism) and each is SSRF-guarded.

scrapeLimit.description: Maximum results to scrape into full markdown (default 3, clamped 1-10).

name: web_sitemap
description: Parse a website's sitemap.xml and return the list of discovered URLs. The sitemap is fetched from {origin}/sitemap.xml. Use this for site enumeration, content auditing, or finding all pages on a domain before selective scraping.

url.description: The http/https URL whose sitemap.xml to fetch. The sitemap is looked up at the URL's origin.

limit.description: Maximum number of URLs to return (default 5000).

name: web_check_links
description: Check whether a list of URLs are reachable by sending HEAD requests in parallel (bounded concurrency). Returns HTTP status code and reason phrase for each URL. Use this to validate links, detect broken pages, or audit site health. SSRF-guarded on every URL.

urls.description: List of http/https URLs to check.

name: browser_scrape
description: Navigate to a URL using a real headless browser (Chrome/Chromium via CDP) and return its content. Unlike web_scrape, this executes JavaScript — use it for SPAs, lazy-loaded content, pages that require JS to render, or when you need to interact with the page before extracting content. Requires Chrome/Chromium to be installed (see --browser-path). SSRF-guarded: only public http/https URLs are allowed. Each concurrent browser page consumes ~50-200 MB RAM; concurrency is bounded by --browser-max-pages.

url.description: The http/https URL to navigate to. Must resolve to a public address.

formats.description: Content formats to return (default ['markdown']). 'screenshot' returns a base64 PNG image block alongside any text blocks.

onlyMainContent.description: Isolate the main/article region before conversion (default false).

waitFor.description: CSS selector to wait for before extracting content. Useful for SPAs that render asynchronously.

waitTimeoutMs.description: Milliseconds to wait for waitFor selector before proceeding anyway (default 5000).

javascript.description: JavaScript snippet to evaluate in the page after navigation and before content extraction. Errors are silently ignored.

scrollToBottom.description: Scroll to the bottom of the page before extracting content (default false). Useful for triggering infinite-scroll / lazy-loaded content.

name: browser_screenshot
description: Navigate to a URL using a real headless browser and return a PNG screenshot as a base64-encoded MCP image content block. Use this when you need a visual representation of a page — to debug layout, capture charts/diagrams, or verify rendering of JS-heavy pages. Requires Chrome/Chromium (see --browser-path). SSRF-guarded.

fullPage.description: Capture the full scrollable page height instead of just the viewport (default false).

width.description: Viewport width in pixels (default 1280, clamped to 3840).

height.description: Viewport height in pixels (default 800, clamped to 2160).

waitFor.description: CSS selector to wait for before taking the screenshot.

waitTimeoutMs.description: Milliseconds to wait for waitFor selector (default 5000).
